title: Houses for rent in Westlake, OH
metaTitle: Houses for Rent in Westlake, OH | Rent Finder
metaDescription: Searching houses for rent in Westlake Ohio? See rent benchmarks from two market sources, transit, utilities, and Section 8 voucher rules for this suburb.
secondaryKeywords: west cuyahoga county houses for rent
answerBox: Westlake is a Cuyahoga County suburb about 12 miles west of downtown Cleveland, between Rocky River and Avon along the Lake Erie corridor. Rent Finder Cleveland's rental portfolio is concentrated on Cleveland's east and southeast side, so we don't currently have confirmed listings in Westlake — contact our team to tell us what you're looking for.

sections.h2: Renting a house in Westlake, Ohio
body: Westlake is a Cuyahoga County suburb roughly 12 miles west of downtown Cleveland, sitting between Rocky River to the east and the Lorain County line (Avon, Avon Lake) to the west. It's one of the larger, more built-out suburbs along that side of the county, among the 59 municipalities that make up Cuyahoga County.

Rent Finder Cleveland's rental homes are concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast side neighborhoods, with additional homes on Cleveland's west side and in Akron, Elyria, and Lorain. We don't currently have confirmed listings in Westlake itself — we'd rather be upfront about that than let a search result overpromise.

[Contact our team](/p/book-showing) and tell us what you're looking for, or check our [full Cleveland listings](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) to see where our current homes are located. Every home in our portfolio, wherever it sits, accepts Section 8 vouchers and is kept HUD-inspection-ready.

sections.h2: What does it cost to rent a house near Westlake?
body: Westlake isn't tracked as its own line item in the rent reports we use, and suburban houses here often run above citywide Cleveland figures given the area's newer housing stock and larger lot sizes. Two different market reports illustrate why it helps to check more than one source: Zumper reports median asking rents, while RentCafe reports averages weighted toward larger, professionally-managed buildings.

Zumper's report, dated July 4, 2026, put the Cleveland-area median at $1,250/mo, with 2-bedroom homes averaging around $1,100 and 3-bedroom homes around $1,350. RentCafe's (Yardi) report, dated July 2, 2026, put the broader average noticeably higher, at $1,564/mo across roughly 787 square feet. Neither figure is Westlake-specific, but together they bracket a reasonable range to expect before you start touring.

sections.h2: Getting around Westlake and commuting for work
body: Westlake is within Cuyahoga County and therefore inside the GCRTA service area, which operates suburban bus routes alongside its Red, Blue, Green, and Waterfront rail lines that all meet at Tower City downtown. Renters commuting from Westlake to downtown Cleveland or University Circle should check current GCRTA route coverage directly, since suburban bus service varies by street.

Westlake's location also matters for commute planning: it sits closer to west-side job centers and to Cleveland Hopkins-area traffic patterns than to University Circle, where the region's two largest employers, Cleveland Clinic and University Hospitals, are based. Renters weighing a Westlake address against an east-side commute should factor in the extra cross-town drive time, especially during peak highway congestion on I-90 or Route 2.

Electric service in Westlake generally comes from The Illuminating Company, since Cleveland Public Power is a municipal utility specific to the City of Cleveland itself. Natural gas in most of the Cleveland area now runs through Enbridge Gas Ohio, which completed its purchase of the former Dominion East Ohio system in March 2024, though a handful of suburbs still use Columbia Gas of Ohio — worth confirming for a specific address. Cleveland Water supplies many surrounding suburbs in addition to the city itself, and sewer/stormwater treatment across the metro generally runs through the Northeast Ohio Regional Sewer District. Confirm the exact providers for your address before budgeting move-in costs.

Westlake is in Cuyahoga County, so Housing Choice Vouchers there are processed through the Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA), the same agency that serves Cleveland. Applications run through [CMHA's Housing Choice Voucher page](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php), which accepts preliminary applications year-round and selects applicants through periodic random drawings rather than a fixed queue.

As with most of Cuyahoga County's western suburbs, Westlake has not passed a local ordinance protecting voucher holders from source-of-income discrimination; Ohio has no statewide protection either. That means a Westlake landlord can lawfully decline a Section 8 applicant, unlike in the handful of suburbs (Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, University Heights, Warrensville Heights, and Linndale, per Signal Cleveland reporting) that have local protections. Every home Rent Finder Cleveland manages accepts vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready regardless of location; our [Cleveland Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) covers the program in more detail.

Westlake is a separate municipality from the City of Cleveland, so ordinances written specifically for Cleveland don't automatically extend here. Cleveland's Lead-Safe Certification law is a good example: it only applies to rental units within Cleveland's city limits, not to Westlake homes. Federal law still requires disclosure of known lead-based paint hazards in any home built before 1978, no matter which city or suburb it's in.

Ohio's statewide landlord-tenant law (Ohio Revised Code Chapter 5321) applies the same way in Westlake as it does in Cleveland proper — including the general rule that security deposits must be returned, with an itemized statement of any deductions, within 30 days of move-out. This is informational only, not legal advice; always verify the current statute before signing a lease.
Renters looking near Westlake often compare these neighboring communities:

[Houses for rent in Avon, OH](/cleveland-rentals/suburbs/houses-for-rent-avon-ohio/) — just west, across the Lorain County line
[Houses for rent in North Olmsted, OH](/cleveland-rentals/suburbs/houses-for-rent-north-olmsted-ohio/) — southeast, further inland in Cuyahoga County

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland have houses for rent in Westlake?
faq.a: Not currently. Our portfolio is concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast side, with a smaller west-side presence and homes in Akron, Elyria, and Lorain. Contact our team to tell us what you're looking for and we'll let you know if that changes or suggest comparable options in the meantime.

faq.q: How much does it cost to rent a house in Westlake?
faq.a: Westlake isn't tracked separately in the reports we use. As general Cleveland-area context, Zumper's July 2026 report shows a median rent of $1,250/mo, while RentCafe's July 2026 report shows an average of $1,564/mo for professionally-managed rentals — suburban houses in Westlake often run above both figures given the local housing stock.

faq.q: Are Section 8 vouchers protected by law in Westlake?
faq.a: No. Westlake has not passed a local source-of-income protection ordinance, and Ohio has no statewide law requiring landlords to accept vouchers. A Westlake landlord can lawfully decline a Section 8 applicant as of this writing, so voucher holders should confirm acceptance with each landlord directly.

faq.q: Which housing authority handles Section 8 in Westlake?
faq.a: Westlake is in Cuyahoga County, so vouchers are processed through the Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA), the same agency that covers Cleveland and its Cuyahoga County suburbs, with year-round preliminary applications submitted online and periodic random drawings used to build the active waitlist.

faq.q: How far is Westlake from downtown Cleveland?
faq.a: Westlake is about 12 miles west of downtown Cleveland, between Rocky River and the Lorain County line near Avon. It sits closer to west-side job centers than to the University Circle medical corridor on the east side, which is worth factoring into a commute.
